title: Praise My Soul the King of Heaven
author: Henry Francis Lyte
story: Lyte paraphrased Psalm 103 into this magnificent hymn of praise. It was sung at the wedding of Princess Elizabeth (later Queen Elizabeth II) in 1947 and remains a favorite at royal occasions.

lines: Praise, my soul, the King of heaven
To His feet thy tribute bring
Ransomed, healed, restored, forgiven, Evermore His praises sing: Alleluia
Alleluia
Praise the everlasting King
Praise Him for His grace and favor To our fathers in distress
Praise Him still the same as ever, Slow to chide, and swift to bless
Alleluia
Alleluia
Glorious in His faithfulness
Father like He tends and spares us
Well our feeble frame He knows
In His hands He gently bears us, Rescues us from all our foes
Alleluia
Alleluia
Widely yet His mercy flows
Frail as summer's flower we flourish, Blows the wind and it is gone
But while mortals rise and perish Our God lives unchanging on, Praise Him, Praise Him, Hallelujah Praise the High Eternal One
Angels, help us to adore Him
Ye behold Him face to face
Sun and moon, bow down before Him, Dwellers all in time and space
Alleluia
Alleluia
Praise with us the God of grace
